title: World Cup Round of 32 picks, predictions: Best bets for every match, including Portugal-Croatia
original_text:
World Cup Round of 32 picks, predictions: Best bets for every match, including Portugal-Croatia
As the World Cup Round of 32 takes shape, a reminder about how betting works in the knockout stage, because it's a mistake everyone has made at some point.
By default, most wagers are for regulation time only. In other words, extra time and penalty shootouts don't count toward most bets. Having said that, some books have started offering bets that explicitly include extra time (or overtime), so beware of what you're betting.
Advertisement
If you want to pick a team to reach the next round, that bet is typically called "to advance" or "to qualify." It covers all methods of getting through, whether in regulation, extra time or penalties.
Keeping that in mind, here are my thoughts on each Round-of-32 matchup as they're confirmed. Remember, that Yahoo Sports' Soccer Pick 'Em with FOX ONE reopens as well for the knockout round — so be sure to make your picks!
Thursday, July 2
Spain vs. Austria
Spain-Austria over 2.5 goals (-115)
In the big picture, the group stage went as anticipated for both these teams. Spain won its group with relative ease. Austria beat minnow Jordan, got Messi-ed by giant Argentina, and drew middle-of-the-pack Algeria.
Advertisement
The paths both teams took were hardly conventional though. Spain opened with a scoreless draw against Cape Verde before crushing Saudi Arabia and handling Uruguay. Austria squeaked through in dramatic fashion, on a goal in the sixth minute of stoppage time against Algeria, barely two minutes after Algeria seemingly knocked Austria out of the tournament.
All that to say that these teams have been who we thought they were so far. Spain hasn't displayed the expected ceiling yet, but also hasn't needed to as its star wingers regain fitness. Spain has room to get better, and Austria's frenetic Red Bull style should only accelerate the game's pace, which would suit Spain just fine and create plenty of opportunities for one or both teams.
Advertisement
Croatia double chance (+100) vs Portugal
Choose your aging superstar! 41-year-old Cristiano Ronaldo and 40-year-old Luka Modric are each still vital to his team's hopes of making a run. Modric may be more important for Croatia, though Ronaldo should be, given that he played every minute of the group stage, looking unnecessary in the opener before scoring twice against Uzbekistan.
Portugal is the more talented team, with possibly the best midfield in the tournament. But that didn't translate into many scoring chances against Congo DR or Colombia. As at the previous World Cup, the Croatians are probably overly reliant on Modric and the 30-somethings, but they somehow keep winning and advancing, having reached the last two World Cup semifinals. They scored twice in a shootout against England, grinded out a 1-0 win over Panama, and then won a rock fight with Ghana. Their numbers don't look great on the whole (minus 1.6 xG in the group stage), but that stems from getting blitzed by England.
Advertisement
Under 2.5 goals is tempting, but the price (-140) is less so. I'll take the Rasputin of international soccer to at least get this game to extra time.
Switzerland vs. Algeria
Switzerland win (-110) vs Algeria
It's a cliché, but this Switzerland team is like clockwork, reaching a fourth straight World Cup knockout stage with relative ease. The Swiss have not won a World Cup elimination game since 1938 (yes, 1938), but they're favored to do so here after a quietly dominant group stage performance. Switzerland rolled through Group B with an expected goal difference of plus-3.9, seventh-best in the group stage. Other than the half hour after going up 2-0 on Canada, the Swiss were in control against lesser competition.
Advertisement
Algeria had the expected group stage in many ways, handling Jordan, losing to Argentina and drawing Austria. I like the fight Algeria showed when trailing twice in the group finale. I'm just not sure the depth or top-to-bottom quality is there if/when Switzerland manages to neutralize the wings.
Since the Swiss reliability is a cliché, I'll also keep it simple and take the better team to win in regulation.
Friday, July 3
Australia vs. Egypt
Australia-Egypt over 2.5 goals (+180)
This is the most even matchup in the Round of 32, and the one with which I struggled most to find a play. I initially wanted under 2.5 goals, but I can't justify the minus-220 price. I don't think Australia is much worse than Egypt, but plus-115 to advance isn't good enough. I wanted to play the scoreless first half, but Egypt's first halves have been fairly open.
Advertisement
As I looked at the numbers more, I came around to zagging and taking the over. All three of Egypt's games had at least two goals and at least two-and-a-half expected goals, and both teams were in the top third of the group stage in possession per game, a rough measure of pace.
I may hate this play mid-game, but as soon as one team scores, the game should open up as the trailing team presses for a goal and the leading team looks for opportunities on the counter, which both teams like to do.
Argentina-Cape Verde over 2.5 goals (-160)
With the possible exception of France, Argentina was the most impressive team in the group stage, winning all three games by a combined 8-1 score, with a smothering defensive effort that matched the offensive firepower everyone knew about.
Advertisement
Cape Verde is one of the tournament's great stories. A nation of about 525,000, roughly the population of Kansas City, Missoria, made its first World Cup and became the smallest country ever to reach the World Cup knockout stage. With three draws, Cape Verde took second in its group behind Spain and ahead of two-time champion Uruguay. The Blue Sharks may be running a bit hot, but that's what happens when a keeper like Vozinha is standing on his head.
My heart does not want to pick against Cinderella, and my head says Argentina will continue to roll. I'll sort of split the difference and play the over. Argentina didn't apply the brakes in the group finale against Jordan and probably won't here either. I'll be rooting for Cape Verde to find a goal of its own to go with the couple likely goals from Argentina.
Colombia vs. Ghana
Colombia-Ghana under 2.5 goals (-155)- Friday, July 3
Both these teams gave up little in the group stage, limiting opponents to three goals in six games between them. The underlying numbers aren't as kind, as each team allowed about two-and-a-half expected goals, but that's still in the top third of defenses in the group stage.
Advertisement
Ghana has bought into Carlos Quieroz's steadfast low-block defensive style and executed it well against England and Croatia. In the group stage, Ghana had the second-lowest possession (36%) and third-fewest expected goals (2.1) of the 32 teams that advanced.
Colombia won't have as many transition opportunities as would be preferred, so Los Cafeteros will have more of the ball and must try to pick the lock of Ghana's back line. It won't be easy, but I think they'll get it done, just not with the track-meet style that was prevalent against Portugal despite the scoreless draw. Look for a 1-0 or 2-0 type of win for Colombia.
